topic: YOLOX Object Detection Python Deployment Developer Guide 2026

key_points: Comprehensive getting started guide updated April 22, 2026
Covers environment setup, loading COCO pretrained weights, and running first detection
PyTorch-focused training tutorial for real-time gesture detector
Blueprint for training YOLOX on any custom object detection task

Includes Roboflow dataset integration for fast iteration

stats.fact: YOLOX GitHub repository has approximately 10.5k stars, reflecting strong developer adoption
YOLOX-X achieves 51.2% mAP on COCO test-dev with 68.6ms latency on V100 GPU
YOLOv8m achieves 50.2% mAP vs YOLOXm's 46.9% on COCO, showing newer models have surpassed YOLOX on accuracy
YOLOv9m achieves 51.4% mAP compared to YOLOXl's 49.7%, despite having fewer than half the parameters
YOLOX-Nano has ~0.91M parameters and achieves 25.3% mAP, making it viable for mobile deployments
YOLOX-Tiny: ~5.1M parameters, 20.2MB, 1.2B FLOPs on 256x256 RGB input
YOLOX-X: ~99M parameters, 395MB+, 22.5B FLOPs on 256x256 RGB input
YOLO models deployed on Jetson Nano with TensorRT achieve detection latencies of 163-170ms (~6 FPS) for standard models
YOLOX supports 6 export formats: MegEngine, ONNX, TensorRT, ncnn, OpenVINO, and CoreML
YOLOX was introduced in July 2021 by Megvii Research (arXiv:2107.08430) and quickly surpassed YOLOv3-v5 on COCO benchmarks

angles: Complete 2026 deployment guide: from pip install to production inference in under an hour
YOLOX vs YOLO26/YOLOv8: when legacy YOLOX is still the right choice (existing pipelines, custom integrations, TensorRT deployments)
Edge AI focus: optimizing YOLOX with ONNX/TensorRT for Jetson Nano and Raspberry Pi
Custom dataset training walkthrough: COCO/VOC format, Exp file configuration, fine-tuning best practices
